canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, 'Information integration' is consistently framed as a latent ability of models to combine information from multiple sources. However, the scope of this integration is described in two distinct ways. One perspective treats it as an inter-document capability, defining it as the ability to "combine evidence across multiple passages to determine relevance in multi-hop retrieval settings" ('Attention in Large Language Models...'). This framing is associated with tasks that require a model to understand relationships between separate documents. A different framing presents it as a more general, intra-context ability to "combine and utilize information from different parts of a linguistic context... particularly across sentence and clause boundaries" ('Middo...'). In this view, information is seen as hierarchically distributed across scales like words and sentences, and a central consideration is a model's "information integration window." Thus, the construct is used to describe model capabilities ranging from multi-hop reasoning across documents to processing dependencies within a single text.
papers: Middo: Model-Informed Dynamic Data Optimization for EnhancedLLMFine-Tuning via Closed-Loop Learning
Attention in Large Language Models Yields Efficient Zero-Shot Re-Rankers
KOR-Bench: Benchmarking Language Models on Knowledge-Orthogonal Reasoning Tasks
MindSearch: Mimicking Human Minds Elicits Deep AI Searcher

definitions.text: The latent ability to combine evidence across multiple passages to determine relevance in multi-hop retrieval settings.
definitions.paper: Attention in Large Language Models Yields Efficient Zero-Shot Re-Rankers
